compressor_summary

report_cases.name: bearing_report
report_cases.text:
# Engineering Technical Report

- Investigation ID: `710101`
- Upload ID: `eval-bearing-001`
- Status: `completed`

## Executive Summary
Vibration growth with stable pressure and a modest temperature rise points toward bearing wear.

## Anomaly Findings
- severe vibration growth
- stable pressure
- minor temperature increase

## Historical NASA Evidence
- Incident `910101` (93% similarity): NASA-style turbofan degradation trajectory with bearing wear | Root cause: bearing wear

## Archived GitHub Evidence
- https://github.com/example/repo/issues/101 (89% similarity): Archived issue about bearing wear and repeated vibration alarms | Root cause: bearing wear

## Root Cause and Recommendations
- bearing wear (90% confidence)

- Inspect bearing assembly
- Replace worn bearing
- Verify lubrication

## Source Traceability
- NASA telemetry evidence was used for the initial diagnostic stage.
- Archived GitHub issue evidence was used as a second-stage institutional knowledge search.

report_cases.name: compressor_report
report_cases.text:
# Engineering Technical Report

- Investigation ID: `710102`
- Upload ID: `eval-compressor-001`
- Status: `completed`

## Executive Summary
Temperature rise, vibration growth, and RPM instability are consistent with compressor degradation.

## Anomaly Findings
- temperature spike
- vibration spike
- rpm drop

## Historical NASA Evidence
- Incident `910102` (91% similarity): NASA-style turbofan compressor degradation trajectory | Root cause: HPC degradation

## Archived GitHub Evidence
- https://github.com/example/repo/issues/103 (88% similarity): Archived issue about compressor seal degradation | Root cause: compressor seal degradation

## Root Cause and Recommendations
- HPC degradation (88% confidence)

- Inspect high-pressure compressor blades and seals
- Validate maintenance records

## Source Traceability
- NASA telemetry evidence was used for the initial diagnostic stage.
- Archived GitHub issue evidence was used as a second-stage institutional knowledge search.
